competitionName: INFINITUM Spring Series Final - by ECCO
headline: Ege leads birdie charge as five aces light up the season so far
blurb: Five tournaments in, the Cutter & Buck Tour has already delivered 5 hole-in-ones, a tight birdie race, and a handful of players showing remarkable consistency.
body:
Five events into the Cutter & Buck Tour season and the stats are already painting a vivid picture. [Mats Ege](/mats-ege) tops the birdie chart with an impressive 76 birdies, holding a narrow lead over [Adam Andersson](/adam-andersson) on 73 and [Ludvig Eriksson](/ludvig-eriksson-ab2) on 72. Close behind sit [Albin Tidén](/albin-tiden) with 71 and [Lauri Ruuska](/lauri-ruuska) with 70, making the top five almost impossible to separate.

The eagle leaderboard tells its own story. [Calum Scott](/calum-scott) shares the lead with five eagles through the opening five tournaments, while Ruuska is not far behind on four — adding a big-play dimension to his already strong birdie numbers.

Perhaps the most eye-catching stat of the season so far is the number of hole-in-ones. Five players have found the cup in one shot, all at Infinitum Golf in Spain. [Matias Honkala](/matias-honkala), [Theis Poulsen](/theis-poulsen), and Eriksson all aced the 6th hole, [Jamie Tofte Nielsen](/jamie-tofte-nielsen) made his ace on 17, and [Oliver Suhr](/oliver-suhr) did the same on the 4th. That is a remarkable run of aces at a single venue.

Consistency has been just as important as brilliance. Only four players have made the cut in all five events: Ege, Andersson, Tofte Nielsen, and [Fabian Wennerlöf](/fabian-wennerlof). Making every weekend on the Cutter & Buck Tour is no small feat, and those four have shown they can perform week after week regardless of the conditions.
